doc_sources.local_mapping: Use its checkpoint/interrupt concept as a reference for CEO/CRO decision pauses, while keeping local SQLite as the authority.
doc_sources.takeaway: LangGraph positions durable execution, streaming, and human-in-the-loop as core orchestration primitives.
doc_sources.title: LangGraph overview

doc_sources.local_mapping: Matches service-worker signed decision guard plus apply-preflight blocker.
doc_sources.takeaway: Tool calls can be paused by policy and resumed after a human decision.
doc_sources.title: LangChain Human-in-the-loop

doc_sources.local_mapping: Keep our trace_events/outcomes/artifacts as the lightweight history before any Temporal adoption.
doc_sources.takeaway: Durable execution treats long-running work as crash-proof execution with persisted history.
doc_sources.title: Temporal durable execution guide

doc_sources.local_mapping: Use this to split lane manager tasks, service requests, and worker starts in our schema.
doc_sources.takeaway: Workflow definitions, executions, schedules, and handlers are separate concepts.
doc_sources.title: Temporal Workflow documentation

doc_sources.local_mapping: Promote tool/LLM calls to first-class trace events before runtime execution is allowed.
doc_sources.takeaway: Agent steps such as LLM calls, tool executions, and routing decisions can be durably persisted.
doc_sources.title: Restate durable agents

doc_sources.local_mapping: Add idle/lease/resume fields before local worker pool activation.
doc_sources.takeaway: The runtime emphasizes retries, persisted progress, and suspension of idle long-running agents.
doc_sources.title: Restate AI agents use case

doc_sources.local_mapping: Map to seeker, lane manager, CRO/CEO, and service-worker roles.
doc_sources.takeaway: A worker/reviewer/manager escalation flow is an explicit sample shape.
doc_sources.title: Microsoft Agent Framework human-in-the-loop workflow sample

doc_sources.local_mapping: Useful later for eval packaging; blocked until model/API/provider/cost gates are explicit.
doc_sources.takeaway: ADK is code-first and includes build/evaluate/deploy framing.
doc_sources.title: Google ADK Python repository

doc_sources.local_mapping: Reinforces that our task DAG and dispatch docket should support dynamic dependencies.
doc_sources.takeaway: Static orchestration is called out as breaking down at scale.
doc_sources.title: CrewAI dynamic agent dependency graph issue

doc_sources.local_mapping: Keep code workers behind sandbox execution and GitHub public-action gates.
doc_sources.takeaway: Coding-agent harnesses need repo, issue, token, and public-action boundaries.
doc_sources.title: OpenHands repository

repo_metadata_source: Public GitHub repository metadata read via https://api.github.com/repos/{owner}/{repo}

repos.description: Framework for orchestrating role-playing, autonomous AI agents.
Code-first Python toolkit for building, evaluating, and deploying sophisticated AI agents.
Framework for building, orchestrating and deploying AI agents and multi-agent workflows.
Platform for building resilient applications that tolerate infrastructure faults.
